The mystery spinner is struggling with his lengths and has been taken to the cleaners, which is not a good sign for India ahead of the final against New Zealand.

description: Non-subsidised LPG, the one that common household users other than the Ujjwala beneficiaries use in their kitchens, will now cost ₹913 per 14.2 kg cylinder in Delhi, according to the Indian Oil Corporation (IOC) website; the price increase is effective from March 7

'Me bas fielding pe cricket nahi khela': Amir launches brutal attack on Kaif
description: Mohammad Amir and Mohammad Kaif are engaged in a verbal spat during the T20 World Cup 2026. Kaif accused Amir of seeking attention by criticizing India, while Amir retorted by questioning Kaif's career stats and suggesting he owes his success to Sourav Ganguly. Amir clarified his comments were opinions, not personal attacks.

title: Morning Digest: Domestic LPG cylinder price hiked by ₹60 amid West Asia conflict; RSP heads for clean sweep in Nepal polls, and more
description: Here is a select list of stories to start the day

title: South Delhi home horror: Woman 'kills' 2 daughters, attempts suicide
description: In a shocking Malviya Nagar incident, police found two sisters dead and their mother unconscious. Authorities suspect the mother, Sunita, killed her daughters, Radhika and Gunisha, before attempting suicide. Crushed naphthalene balls and a crepe bandage were recovered from the scene. Investigations into potential depression and domestic discord are ongoing.

title: 'It felt like small tremor': 21 injured as mosque balcony wall collapses in Jaipur
description: A balcony wall at Firdaus Masjid in Jaipur collapsed shortly after Friday prayers, injuring at least 21 people. The incident occurred around 2:30 pm as devotees were exiting the mosque. Preliminary investigations suggest structural issues, possibly exacerbated by worshippers leaning on the aging, single-brick structure.

'Red corridor' will be crushed by March 31, says Amit Shah
description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah announced that left-wing extremism will be completely wiped out by March 31, ending the ambition of a "red corridor" from Tirupati to Pashupati. Speaking at CISF's raising day, he lauded security forces for curbing Naxal activities and launched housing projects for the paramilitary force.

Sam Altman at Adda: ‘I don’t think there should be any single superintelligence in the world’
Nitish Kumar firm on Rajya Sabha move, son Nishant to join JDU tomorrow
description: Nishant Kumar, son of Bihar CM Nitish Kumar, is set to join the JDU on March 8, marking his political debut. Nitish Kumar has affirmed his decision to move to the Rajya Sabha is final and irreversible, assuring party members he will remain involved in Bihar's development. Nishant's entry sparks speculation about his future role within the party or government.

title: Explained: Not just oil and gas, the West Asia conflict is hitting core Indian industries
'Lost to Australia in 2023': Akhtar says Ahmedabad pressure will haunt India in T20 WC final
description: Former Pakistan cricketers Shoaib Akhtar and Umar Gul highlighted the immense pressure on India ahead of the T20 World Cup 2026 final against New Zealand.  They cited the weight of fan expectations and India's recent struggles at the Narendra Modi Stadium, including losses in major finals, as potential challenges for the home team.

title: 'Headless batting in semi-final': R Ashwin’s brutal verdict on Abhishek Sharma
description: Ravichandran Ashwin backs young opener Abhishek Sharma for the T20 World Cup final despite his struggles, urging the team to persist with him. Ashwin, however, advised Abhishek to better understand match situations and avoid 'headless batting,' emphasizing timing over brute force. He stressed keeping the talented opener in a good mental space for the crucial clash.

Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding plans shaken by Matty Healy’s wedding
description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have billions of fans around the world waiting for the two to tie the knot in the next few months. But it seems the billionaire pop star, Taylor Swift, might be facing a few strong emotions about her ex boyfriend, Matty Healy, after strong rumors suggest that he is also tying the knot in the next few months.

title: Knicks vs Nuggets injury report, lineups, head-to-head and key stats
description: The Knicks, battling injuries to key guards like Miles McBride, face the Nuggets in Denver on March 6, 2026. Despite recent success against Denver, New York's backcourt depth is tested. The Nuggets, also missing frontcourt players Aaron Gordon and Peyton Watson, aim to leverage home-court advantage. This crucial matchup sees star big men Nikola Jokic and Karl-Anthony Towns go head-to-head.

title: In a Bengal college, an Iftar party to be held by women for women
description: The event, called ‘2026 Dosti ki Iftar’ and being organised by the Deeniyat Muallima College (located in Santragachi, across the river from Kolkata) and the city-based initiative Know Your Neighbour, intends to “provide a space for women to exchanges their views, and break myths and stereotypes”.

title: LPG price hike: Domestic, commercial cylinders to get costlier from today — report
description: Domestic cooking gas prices are set to rise from Saturday, with a 14.2-kg LPG cylinder increasing by Rs 60 nationwide. Commercial cylinders will also see a Rs 115 hike. This marks the first significant domestic LPG price change since April 2025. Despite global energy concerns, the government assures comfortable energy supply and diversified import sources.

title: Andhra Pradesh firecracker unit blast: Gone in a flash fire
description: An explosion at a firecracker manufacturing unit in Vetlapalem village of Kakinada district, Andhra Pradesh, claimed 28 workers’ lives, including eight women. Preliminary investigations reveal that the unit’s owners had allegedly stored explosive materials far beyond the permitted limit and engaged 31 workers, nearly four times the number allowed under licence, in a bid to fulfil festival and wedding orders, reports T. Appala Naidu

title: Miami Heat vs Hornets injury report: Full list, H2H, team stats
description: The Miami Heat face the Charlotte Hornets on March 6, 2026, with playoff positioning on the line. Miami, holding a slight edge in the standings and season series, will be without key players like Terry Rozier and Norman Powell, impacting their rotation. Charlotte, seeking a seventh straight win, relies on strong home defense and perimeter shooting.

title: Phansi-ghar case: Kejriwal appears before House panel

title: Probe on after failed burglary attempt at bank
Mic-drop mandate as Nepal grooves to rapper Balen’s beat
description: Nepal's political landscape has been dramatically reshaped as the Rastriya Swatantra Party, spearheaded by Balen Shah, achieved a stunning victory in the parliamentary elections. This generational shift has seen veteran leaders facing significant defeats, with the urban electorate overwhelmingly backing the new force. The results signal a powerful rejection of the old political order.

We shocked quite a few people at Los Angeles; they didn’t expect that from Indian athletics: Valsamma
description: From humble beginnings to making the world take notice in the 1980s, Valsamma had a storied career as a top athlete. She speaks in this interview about the hurdles moment that changed her life, her significant role in the revolution of women’s athletics, why Kerala is not as dominant as it once was, and what could make a difference at the grassroots level

title: Nine held for abducting man over land deal issue
Having conversations on AI’s role in Wikipedia, says founder Jimmy Wales
description: Jimmy Wales says AI can contribute by making translations better but warns that “even a good machine translation” can fall short of expectations if “cultural questions” are not addressed

title: Youth, student groups criticise Karnataka Budget over jobs, education funding
description: Student and youth organisations have criticised the State Budget for 2026-27, alleging that it fails to address the pressing issues of unemployment among youth and the declining state of public education

title: 'Sholay' rerun: Girl climbs mobile tower in Bihar, demands lover’s release from police custody
description: In a gripping scenario in Bihar, a young woman, just 20 years old, scaled a telecommunications tower, insistent on the release of her boyfriend, who had been taken into custody after her family lodged an abduction complaint. The tense standoff stretched on for hours as the police negotiated, finally bringing the boyfriend to the scene.

title: Lakshya Sen after entering All England semis: ‘Was extra prepared after long rallies… didn’t give any easy mistakes immediately after’
Beyond the uterus: What rare male endometriosis cases reveal about the disease
US says India’s Russian oil waiver short-term step to stabilise global prices
description: The US has granted India a temporary waiver to buy Russian oil, aiming to stabilize global prices amid Middle East supply disruptions. This short-term measure allows India to access millions of barrels of Russian crude currently at sea, easing immediate pressure on refineries worldwide.

title: Delhi's Uttam Nagar on edge after Holi killing: CRPF deployed amid heighted communal tensions
description: Two days after a fatal Holi dispute in Delhi's Uttam Nagar, a car and bike, allegedly belonging to an accused's relative, were torched, escalating tensions. Heavy security is now in place as police investigate the vehicle burning and have arrested eight individuals in connection with the killing.

description: A Bengaluru man, initially reported to have died of a heart attack, was actually smothered to death. Police exhumed his body, revealing the truth. His wife and her alleged lover have been arrested for the murder, orchestrated after the victim discovered their affair. The wife then falsely claimed a heart attack to conceal the crime.

Inside the paradox of Mexico's never-ending war on drugs
description: The decades-long war on drugs, initiated by Nixon, has devolved into a geopolitical quagmire. The recent death of cartel leader El Mencho, hailed as a victory, quickly led to widespread chaos, demonstrating how successes in combating drug trafficking often breed deeper crises and more violence across Latin America.

title: Motorola Edge 70 Fusion Review | Sensible option for users who prioritise longevity and practicality
description: Motorola Edge 70 Fusion enters a competitive segment where buyers expect a strong balance between design, performance, and battery life

title: Microsoft becomes first company to say it is not 'abandoning' Anthropic
description: Microsoft will continue using Anthropic's AI models, including Claude, for its customers despite the US Department of War labeling the startup a supply-chain risk.  The tech giant's legal team reviewed the designation, concluding most clients can still access the technology. This decision comes after the Pentagon's move to phase out Anthropic's systems, sparking debate over AI in defense.

title: Bhopal teacher dies after consuming bhang at Holi gathering with students
description: A 26-year-old teaching faculty member died under suspicious circumstances after allegedly consuming bhang during a Holi gathering with students. Krishna Nayak reportedly consumed multiple glasses of bhang, leading to him feeling unwell and later dying at a hospital. The exact cause of death is pending a post-mortem report.
